commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7dde751104111a7454e3c1f5d771e4637ed6fc6d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes WebChat Gateway chat delivery so agent-run TTS/media final payloads can be broadcast as final `chat` events and adds focused handler plus running-gateway regression tests.

PR surface: Source +39, Tests +241. Total +280 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main appends agent-run media to transcript state but has no normal final WebChat broadcast for non-source agent media finals. The PR's own block-then-final tests also expose the relevant ordering path, though I did not execute tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The PR needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof and maintainer review of the final/history media alignment; this is not safe to hand to merge automation as-is.

target.title: fix(webchat): broadcast agent TTS media final payloads
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes #81468.
- Bridges WebChat-managed agent-run TTS/media supplements into the final `chat` broadcast payload, so the live WebChat path receives the audio attachment that was already appended to the transcript.
- Keeps media-bearing `block` replies from being saved as the eventual final broadcast snapshot; only actual `final` deliveries can populate that final media message.
- Preserves transcript dedupe by keeping one assistant-media transcript entry while allowing a later media final to control the live final broadcast.
- Adds running Gateway proof that sends `chat.send` over WebSocket, first delivers a media block, then delivers the final TTS media reply, verifies the assistant-media HTTP route can serve the MP3, and confirms `chat.history` still has one assistant media supplement.

## Verification
- `env COREPACK_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-corepack node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts -t "agent.*media|auto-TTS final media|media-bearing block" --reporter verbose`
  - `Test Files 2 passed (2)`
  - `Tests 10 passed | 166 skipped (176)`
- `env COREPACK_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-corepack node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts -t "chat.send broadcasts agent-run TTS audio over the running gateway" --reporter verbose`
  - `Test Files 1 passed (1)`
  - `Tests 1 passed | 29 skipped (30)`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed
- Agent-run auto-TTS/audio replies could be persisted as a WebChat assistant-media transcript supplement while the live final WebChat `chat` event omitted the audio attachment.
- A media-bearing `block` delivery could be captured as the final media broadcast candidate and mask a later real final media reply.
- TTS spoken text must not be duplicated into the visible webchat final or persisted assistant history.

Real environment tested
- Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS at head `7dde751104111a7454e3c1f5d771e4637ed6fc6d`, based on `origin/main` `23e9bc8c0b61f37851136dc5da2311935e348f5d`.
- Local OpenClaw Gateway server test using the repo's connected Control UI server suite.
- Real local WebSocket client sends `chat.send` to the running Gateway.
- Real local HTTP fetches hit `http://127.0.0.1:<port>/__openclaw__/assistant-media` for metadata and ticketed media download.

Exact steps or command run after this patch
- `env COREPACK_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-corepack node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts -t "chat.send broadcasts agent-run TTS audio over the running gateway" --reporter verbose`
- The mocked agent dispatch starts an agent run, sends a media-bearing `block` reply from `block.mp3`, then sends the final TTS reply from `tts.mp3`.

Evidence after fix
- Vitest reported: `✓ |gateway-server| ... chat.send broadcasts agent-run TTS audio over the running gateway 432ms`.
- Suite summary: `Test Files 1 passed (1)`, `Tests 1 passed | 29 skipped (30)`, `Duration 7.47s`.
- Focused handler regression also reported the following tests passing in both `gateway-client` and `gateway-methods` projects:
  - `persists agent-run audio replies emitted as media-bearing block payloads`
  - `persists auto-TTS final media as audio-only so webchat does not duplicate assistant text`
  - `broadcasts final agent media instead of an earlier media-bearing block`

Observed result after fix
- WebSocket final event arrived with `runId: idem-running-gateway-agent-tts`, `sessionKey: agent:main:main`, and `state: final`.
- Final assistant content included `{ type: "text", text: "Audio reply" }` plus an audio attachment for the final `tts.mp3` path with `kind: "audio"`, `mimeType: "audio/mpeg"`, and `isVoiceNote: true`.
- The final WebSocket message did not contain the model transcript spoken text.
- The earlier `block.mp3` payload did not replace the final `tts.mp3` broadcast.
- Assistant media metadata request returned HTTP 200 with `available: true` and a `v1.` media ticket.
- Ticketed media download returned HTTP 200 and 4 bytes.
- `chat.history` returned exactly one assistant message for the run and did not contain the spoken TTS text.
- Block-only media regression asserted no final broadcast was emitted from a media block by itself.

What was not tested
- No real Microsoft/Xiaomi TTS credentials were used; the test uses a local MP3 fixture while exercising the actual Gateway WebSocket final event, assistant-media HTTP route, ticketed media download, and history dedupe path.
- Browser autoplay behavior was not tested.

## AI Assistance Disclosure
This PR was prepared with AI assistance. I reviewed the changed code path and verified the targeted tests above.
target.author: spacegeologist

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
